input.stepLabel: Trigger refund-reversal on 3DS-enrolled card — expect 3DS challenge presented BEFORE gateway debit
input.errorMessage: RaceConditionError: Gateway debit completed (refund_reversal_id: rrv_47281) BEFORE 3DS challenge callback returned. 3DS challenge presented at t=12.3s, customer authentication completed at t=18.1s, but gateway debit fired at t=11.7s (BEFORE auth complete). State machine reached terminal 'reversed' state without authentication signature.
input.stackTrace:
RaceConditionError: gateway debit fired before 3DS auth complete
  at threeds-orchestrator.ts:218 onChallengePresented()
  at threeds-orchestrator.ts:142 invokeChallenge()
  at refund.reversal.service.ts:67 executeReversal()
kbContext: 3DS (3-D Secure) authentication is asynchronous — the challenge is presented to the user, and the auth result returns via webhook minutes later. The reversal state machine MUST await the auth result before dispatching the gateway debit.
Iksula 3DS orchestrator uses an event-driven state machine: PENDING_AUTH → AUTH_COMPLETE → DISPATCH_DEBIT → REVERSED. Skipping any state is a race-condition bug.
The reversal service has a fast-path optimization that bypasses 3DS for trusted-device reversals. This flag may be incorrectly set in staging-iksula seed data.

groundTruth.rootCauseDetail: Refund-reversal state machine in refund.reversal.service.ts:67 dispatches gateway debit synchronously without awaiting the 3DS auth-complete event. The flakiness (40% pass rate) suggests timing-dependent ordering — when challenge response is fast, dispatch fires before debit, when slow, debit fires first. Fix: convert executeReversal() to await the 3DS auth-complete event before calling dispatch.
groundTruth.notesForEval: Top-2 hit on race-condition, code-bug, or payment-gateway. race-condition is the canonical label (40% flake rate is a smoking gun); code-bug is the under-layer (missing await); payment-gateway captures the integration-protocol aspect. All three acceptable. Sherlock should ideally cite refund.reversal.service.ts:67 and reference the state-machine KB chunk.
